# azurefoundry_agent_v2 (Resource)

Manages an Azure AI Foundry Agent (v2 API).

The v2 API supports prompt agents (LLM + tools) and hosted agents (`kind = "hosted"` or `"container_app"`) where you ship a container image that speaks the Foundry agent protocols. Most fields are common to both kinds; the hosted-only fields (`image`, `cpu`, `memory`, `container_protocol_versions`, `environment_variables`) are ignored when `kind = "prompt"`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Prompt agent with file search over a vector store.
resource "azurefoundry_agent_v2" "researcher" {
  name         = "tf-researcher"
  kind         = "prompt"
  model        = "gpt-4o-mini"
  instructions = "You are a Terraform specialist. Cite file:line for every claim."

  tools {
    type             = "file_search"
    vector_store_ids = [azurefoundry_vector_store_v2.docs.id]
    max_num_results  = 20
  }

  tools {
    type = "code_interpreter"
    code_interpreter = {
      file_ids = [azurefoundry_file_v2.style_guide.id]
    }
  }

  metadata = {
    environment = "dev"
  }
}

# Hosted agent: ship a container that speaks the Foundry Responses protocol.
# The agent's managed identity is exposed via instance_identity for RBAC.
resource "azurefoundry_agent_v2" "fraud_detection" {
  name         = "fraud-detection"
  kind         = "hosted"
  model        = "gpt-4o-mini"
  image        = "myacr.azurecr.io/fraud-agent:0.1.0"
  cpu          = "1"
  memory       = "2Gi"
  instructions = "Triage incoming transactions; escalate when score > 0.85."

  container_protocol_versions = [
    {
      protocol = "responses"
      version  = "v1"
    }
  ]

  environment_variables = {
    LOG_LEVEL = "info"
  }

  warmup         = true
  warmup_timeout = "5m"
}

# Grant the hosted agent's managed identity Azure AI User on the Foundry account.
resource "azurerm_role_assignment" "fraud_agent_runtime" {
  scope                = data.azurerm_cognitive_account.foundry.id
  role_definition_name = "Azure AI User"
  principal_id         = azurefoundry_agent_v2.fraud_detection.instance_identity.principal_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `kind` (String) Agent kind. One of `prompt` (LLM + tools, no container), `hosted` / `container_app` (you ship a container image), or `workflow` (experimental). The hosted-only fields are required when `kind` is `hosted` or `container_app`, ignored otherwise.
- `model` (String) Model deployment name (e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`). For prompt agents this is the underlying LLM; for hosted agents it's the runtime model your container talks to via the Foundry-managed identity.
- `name` (String) User-supplied agent name. Unique within the Foundry project. Changing this forces replacement.

### Optional

- `container_protocol_versions` (Attributes List) Protocols the container speaks. Required for `container_app`/`hosted` kinds. Today the valid protocols are `responses` (Azure OpenAI Responses API) and `a2a` (Agent-to-Agent). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--container_protocol_versions))
- `cpu` (String) vCPU allocation as a string (e.g. `1`, `2`). Required for `container_app`/`hosted` kinds. Allowed pairs: see the Foundry hosted-agent size matrix.
- `description` (String) Human-readable description, surfaced in the Foundry portal.
- `environment_variables` (Map of String) Env vars injected into the hosted agent container. Do not put secrets here — use a connection to a secret store instead.
- `image` (String) Container image URL including tag. Required when `kind` is `container_app` or `hosted`; ignored for `prompt`. Example: `myacr.azurecr.io/fraud-agent:0.1.0`.
- `instructions` (String) System prompt for the agent. Ignored for hosted agents — the container defines its own behavior.
- `memory` (String) Memory allocation in GiB as a string (e.g. `2Gi`, `4Gi`). Required for `container_app`/`hosted` kinds.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Arbitrary key/value labels stored alongside the agent. Up to 16 entries.
- `structured_inputs_json` (String) JSON string describing the agent's `structured_inputs` schema. Use `jsonencode({...})` in HCL or `json.dumps({...})` in a Pulumi program.
- `tools` (Block List) Tools enabled for the agent. Order is preserved on the wire. Set the variant block (`code_interpreter`, `function`, `mcp`, ...) matching the `type`; the others are ignored. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tools))
- `warmup` (Boolean) When `true` on a `kind="hosted"` agent, Create blocks until the per-session sandbox responds non-424 on its Responses endpoint. Lets dependent resources rely on the agent being immediately reachable. No-op for `kind="prompt"`. Defaults to `false`.
- `warmup_timeout` (String) Maximum time to wait for the agent to become ready when `warmup=true`. Go duration string (e.g. `5m`, `90s`). Defaults to `5m`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Unix timestamp (seconds) when the latest version of the agent was created.
- `id` (String) Foundry-assigned agent ID. Stable for the lifetime of the agent.
- `instance_identity` (Attributes) Foundry-managed Entra identity for a hosted-agent version. Computed. Use `client_id` / `principal_id` to grant runtime RBAC (e.g. `Azure AI User` on the Foundry account). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instance_identity))
- `version` (String) Foundry-assigned version identifier for the latest definition. Increments on each Update.

<a id="nestedatt--container_protocol_versions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `container_protocol_versions`

Required:

- `protocol` (String) `responses` or `a2a`.
- `version` (String) Protocol version, e.g. `v1`.


<a id="nestedblock--tools"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools`

Required:

- `type` (String) Tool type. One of `file_search`, `code_interpreter`, `web_search`, `bing_grounding`, `function`, `openapi`, `mcp`, `azure_ai_search`, `memory_search`.

Optional:

- `azure_ai_search` (Attributes) Azure AI Search retrieval. Used when `type = "azure_ai_search"`. One or more `indexes` are queried per request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--azure_ai_search))
- `bing_grounding` (Attributes) Bing Search v7 grounding via a project connection. Used when `type = "bing_grounding"`. For the managed Foundry-hosted variant that needs no connection, use `type = "web_search"`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--bing_grounding))
- `code_interpreter` (Attributes) Sandboxed Python execution. Used when `type = "code_interpreter"`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--code_interpreter))
- `function` (Attributes) OpenAI-style function calling. Used when `type = "function"`. `parameters_json` is a JSON Schema describing the function's arguments. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--function))
- `max_num_results` (Number) Maximum search hits returned per query. Used when `type = "file_search"`. Defaults to the Foundry server-side default.
- `mcp` (Attributes) Model Context Protocol server. Used when `type = "mcp"`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--mcp))
- `memory_search` (Attributes) Attach a Foundry Memory store (preview). Used when `type = "memory_search"`. The wire spelling is `memory_search_preview` while the feature is in preview; this provider accepts the shorter `memory_search` and translates at the boundary so consumer HCL stays stable across the GA cut-over. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--memory_search))
- `openapi` (Attributes) OpenAPI spec inlined as a tool. Used when `type = "openapi"`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--openapi))
- `vector_store_ids` (List of String) Vector store IDs to search. Used when `type = "file_search"`.

<a id="nestedatt--tools--azure_ai_search"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.azure_ai_search`

Required:

- `indexes` (Attributes List) Indexes to query. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tools--azure_ai_search--indexes))

<a id="nestedatt--tools--azure_ai_search--indexes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.azure_ai_search.indexes`

Required:

- `index_name` (String) Name of the index within the search account.
- `project_connection_id` (String) Project connection ID for the Azure AI Search account.

Optional:

- `query_type` (String) `simple`, `semantic`, `vector`, `vector_simple_hybrid`, or `vector_semantic_hybrid`. Defaults to Foundry's preference.
- `top_k` (Number) Maximum hits returned per query. Defaults to the server-side default.



<a id="nestedatt--tools--bing_grounding"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.bing_grounding`

Required:

- `connection_id` (String) Project connection ID bound to the Bing Search resource.


<a id="nestedatt--tools--code_interpreter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.code_interpreter`

Optional:

- `file_ids` (List of String) File IDs available to the code interpreter sandbox.


<a id="nestedatt--tools--function"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.function`

Required:

- `name` (String) Function name the model will call.

Optional:

- `description` (String) Human-readable description shown to the model.
- `parameters_json` (String) JSON Schema (as a string) for the function's parameters. Use `jsonencode({...})` in HCL.


<a id="nestedatt--tools--mcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.mcp`

Required:

- `server_label` (String) Display label for the MCP server in tool-call traces.
- `server_url` (String) URL of the MCP server. Must be reachable from Foundry's egress.

Optional:

- `project_connection_id` (String) Project connection ID used to authenticate to the MCP server.
- `require_approval` (String) `always`, `never`, or omitted (Foundry default). Controls whether the user must approve tool invocations before they run.


<a id="nestedatt--tools--memory_search"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.memory_search`

Required:

- `memory_store_name` (String) Name of the `azurefoundry_memory_store_v2` to attach.

Optional:

- `scope` (String) Memory scope expression. Use `"{{$userId}}"` to resolve from the `x-memory-user-id` header or the caller's Entra identity.
- `update_delay` (Number) Seconds of inactivity before extracted memories are written back to the store. Defaults to 300.


<a id="nestedatt--tools--openapi"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tools.openapi`

Required:

- `name` (String) Tool name surfaced to the model.
- `spec_json` (String) OpenAPI 3.x spec serialized as a JSON string. Use `jsonencode({...})` or `file("spec.json")` in HCL.

Optional:

- `auth_type` (String) `anonymous` (default) or `connection`. When `connection`, Foundry uses the project connection bound to the API host.
- `description` (String) Human-readable description shown to the model.



<a id="nestedatt--instance_identity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instance_identity`

Read-Only:

- `client_id` (String) Application (client) ID of the agent's managed identity.
- `principal_id` (String) Object (principal) ID of the agent's managed identity. Use this in role assignments.
